accessibility.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Fix the color contrast: set body background-color to #ffffff and color to #000000
  2. Improve readability: set body font-size to 1rem and line-height to 1.6
  3. Add a visible focus indicator: set a:focus outline to 2px solid orange
accessibility.starter:
Home | About | Contact

Welcome

This page demonstrates accessible CSS styling.
accessibility.solution:
Home | About | Contact

Welcome

This page demonstrates accessible CSS styling.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Set a:focus outline to 2px solid orange

commonMistakes.msg: Never remove focus outlines with outline: none - keyboard users rely on them to see which element is focused.
Use rem instead of px for font-size. This allows users to scale text in their browser settings.

align.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Center the div horizontally using margin: auto (it already has a width)
  2. Center the text inside the div using text-align: center
  3. Center the img by making it display: block with margin: auto
align.starter:
Centered Content

This box and its text are centered.
align.solution:
Centered Content

This box and its text are centered.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Make the img a block element

commonMistakes.msg: text-align doesn't work on images. To center an image, make it display: block and use margin: auto.

attribute_selectors.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Style all links with a target attribute: use a[target] and set color to red
  2. Style links where target equals _blank: use a[target="_blank"] and set background-color to yellow
  3. Style inputs of type text: use input[type="text"] and set border to 2px solid blue
attribute_selectors.starter:
Normal link
Self target link
Blank target link

Name:
attribute_selectors.solution:
Normal link
Self target link
Blank target link

Name:

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use square brackets for attribute selectors: a[target], not a dot (that's for classes).

background.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. In the body selector, set background-color to lightblue
  2. In the body selector, set background-image to url('img_tree.png')
  3. In the body selector, set background-repeat to no-repeat
  4. In the body selector, set background-position to right top
background.starter:
CSS Background

A background color and a background image can be set together. The color shows where the image does not cover.
background.solution:
CSS Background

A background color and a background image can be set together. The color shows where the image does not cover.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use the individual property background-image for this challenge, not the shorthand background.
Wrap the image path in url(): background-image: url('img_tree.png');

border.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Give p.solid a solid border style
  2. Give p.dashed a dashed border style
  3. Give p.thick a border using the shorthand: 3px solid red
  4. Give p.rounded a solid border and a border-radius of 5px
border.starter:
A solid border.

A dashed border.

A thick red border.

A rounded border.
border.solution:
A solid border.

A dashed border.

A thick red border.

A rounded border.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Give p.thick a shorthand border: 3px solid red

commonMistakes.msg: For p.thick, use the shorthand border: 3px solid red instead of separate properties.

boxmodel.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the div width to 300px
  2. Add 25px of padding (space inside the border)
  3. Add a border of 5px solid navy
  4. Add 20px of margin (space outside the border)
boxmodel.starter:
The CSS box model wraps every element: margin, border, padding, content.

This box has all four parts of the box model.
boxmodel.solution:
The CSS box model wraps every element: margin, border, padding, content.

This box has all four parts of the box model.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: The margin should be 20px (outside the border), not 25px. The padding is 25px (inside the border).

colors.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the h1 color using a color name: Tomato
  2. Set the h2 color using a hex value: #1e90ff
  3. Set the p color using an rgb value: rgb(60, 179, 113)
colors.starter:
Color Name

Hex Value

RGB Value
colors.solution:
Color Name

Hex Value

RGB Value

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Set h1 color to the name Tomato

commonMistakes.msg: Tomato is a color name, not a hex value. Use it without the # sign: color: Tomato;
Hex values need a # prefix: color: #1e90ff;

combinators.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Use the descendant selector div p to set color to blue
  2. Use the child selector div > p to set font-weight to bold
  3. Use the adjacent sibling selector h2 + p to set color to red
combinators.starter:
Direct child of div.

Nested inside section (still a descendant of div).

Heading

First paragraph after the heading.

Second paragraph after the heading.
combinators.solution:
Direct child of div.

Nested inside section (still a descendant of div).

Heading

First paragraph after the heading.

Second paragraph after the heading.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Use div p to set color to blue
Use div > p to set font-weight to bold
Use h2 + p to set color to red

commonMistakes.msg: The bold style should use the child selector div > p (with >), not the descendant selector div p.

counters.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. On the body, reset a counter named section using counter-reset
  2. On each h2, increment the counter using counter-increment: section
  3. On h2::before, display the counter using content: counter(section) ". "
counters.starter:
CSS Counters

HTML

CSS

JavaScript
counters.solution:
CSS Counters

HTML

CSS

JavaScript

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: The counter must be reset on the body first, then incremented on each h2.
Use the double-colon syntax h2::before for pseudo-elements (CSS3 standard).

css_tooltip.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Position .tooltip as relative and make it inline-block
  2. Position .tooltiptext as absolute and hide it with visibility: hidden
  3. On hover (.tooltip:hover .tooltiptext), set visibility: visible
css_tooltip.starter: Hover over the word hereI am a tooltip! to see the tooltip.
css_tooltip.solution: Hover over the word hereI am a tooltip! to see the tooltip.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Tooltips use visibility: hidden/visible instead of display: none/block so the tooltip keeps its dimensions for positioning.

display.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make the li elements display inline (horizontal navigation)
  2. Make the span elements display as block
  3. Hide the element with class .hidden using display: none
display.starter:
• Home
  • About
  • Contact

These spans are now block elements.

This paragraph is hidden.
display.solution:
• Home
  • About
  • Contact

These spans are now block elements.

This paragraph is hidden.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use display: none to remove the element completely. visibility: hidden hides it but still takes up space.

dropdowns.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Position the .dropdown as relative
  2. Position the .dropdown-content as absolute and hide it with display: none
  3. On hover (.dropdown:hover .dropdown-content), show it with display: block
dropdowns.starter:
Link 1
Link 2
Link 3
dropdowns.solution:
Link 1
Link 2
Link 3

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: The dropdown content should be position: absolute (positioned relative to its parent). The parent .dropdown should be position: relative.

flexbox_container.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .container justify-content to space-between
  2. Set the .container align-items to center
  3. Set the .container flex-wrap to wrap
flexbox_container.starter:
1
2
3
4
flexbox_container.solution:
1
2
3
4

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use space-between to distribute items with equal space between them, not center.

flexbox_intro.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .container display to flex
  2. Set the .container flex-direction to row
  3. Set the .container gap to 10px
flexbox_intro.starter:
1
2
3
flexbox_intro.solution:
1
2
3

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use display: flex for a block-level flex container. inline-flex makes the container inline.

flexbox_items.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make all .item elements grow equally with flex: 1
  2. Make #main take up twice the space with flex: 2
  3. Give #sidebar an order of -1 to move it first
flexbox_items.starter:
Main Content
Sidebar
Extra
flexbox_items.solution:
Main Content
Sidebar
Extra

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Set #main to flex: 2 so it takes twice the space of the other items.

flexbox_responsive.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .navbar display to flex and justify-content to space-around
  2. Create a media query for max-width: 600px
  3. Inside the query, change .navbar flex-direction to column and align-items to center
flexbox_responsive.starter: Home About Services Contact
flexbox_responsive.solution: Home About Services Contact

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Inside the query, set flex-direction to column

commonMistakes.msg: Put the flex-direction: column inside the media query, not in the base rule. The navbar should only stack on small screens.

float.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Float the img to the left
  2. Add 15px of margin-right to the img so text doesn't touch it
  3. Use clear: both on the footer so it appears below the floated image
float.starter:
Float and Clear

The float property places an element to the left or right, allowing text to wrap around it. The clear property controls what happens next to floated elements.

Footer - should appear below the image.
float.solution:
Float and Clear

The float property places an element to the left or right, allowing text to wrap around it. The clear property controls what happens next to floated elements.

Footer - should appear below the image.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Clear the float on the footer

commonMistakes.msg: Use clear: both to clear floats from both sides. This is the most common and safest approach.

font.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .serif font-family to "Times New Roman", Times, serif
  2. Set the .sans font-family to Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif
  3. Add a .mono rule: set font-family to "Courier New", Courier, monospace
font.starter:
This is a serif font (Times New Roman).

This is a sans-serif font (Arial).

This is a monospace font (Courier New).
font.solution:
This is a serif font (Times New Roman).

This is a sans-serif font (Arial).

This is a monospace font (Courier New).

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Set .serif font-family to "Times New Roman", Times, serif
Set .sans font-family to Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif
Add a .mono rule with font-family "Courier New", Courier, monospace

commonMistakes.msg: Multi-word font names need quotes: "Times New Roman". Always end with a generic family as fallback.

form_styling.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Style input[type=text]: set width to 100%, padding to 12px, and box-sizing to border-box
  2. Style input[type=submit]: set background-color to #04AA6D, color to white, and border to none
  3. On hover (input[type=submit]:hover), set background-color to #059862

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: When using width: 100% with padding, add box-sizing: border-box so the padding is included in the total width.

grid_container.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .grid grid-template-columns to 1fr 1fr 1fr (three equal columns)
  2. Set grid-template-rows to 100px 100px (two rows of 100px)
  3. Set justify-content to center
grid_container.starter:
1
2
3
4
5
6
grid_container.solution:
1
2
3
4
5
6

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use 1fr 1fr 1fr for equal-width columns. auto sizes columns based on content.

grid_intro.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .grid-container display to grid
  2. Define 3 equal columns using grid-template-columns: auto auto auto
  3. Set the gap to 10px
grid_intro.starter:
1
2
3
4
5
6
grid_intro.solution:
1
2
3
4
5
6

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use display: grid for a grid layout, not display: flex.

grid_item.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make .header span all 3 columns using grid-column: 1 / 4
  2. Make .sidebar span 2 rows using grid-row: 2 / 4
grid_item.starter:
Header
Sidebar
Content 1
Content 2
Content 3
Content 4
grid_item.solution:
Header
Sidebar
Content 1
Content 2
Content 3
Content 4

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: You can also use grid-column: 1 / 4 (line-based) instead of span 3. Both work, but the challenge asks for line numbers.

height_width.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the div width to 200px
  2. Set the div height to 100px
height_width.starter: This div has a specific width and height.
height_width.solution: This div has a specific width and height.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

howto.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Add an external stylesheet by inserting a <link> element with rel "stylesheet" and href "mystyle.css"
  2. Add an internal stylesheet: inside the <style> tag, set h1 color to navy
  3. Add an inline style: on the <p> element, set color to red
howto.starter:
Three Ways to Add CSS

This paragraph uses inline CSS.
howto.solution:
Three Ways to Add CSS

This paragraph uses inline CSS.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Add a <link> element with rel "stylesheet" and href "mystyle.css"
In the <style> tag, set h1 color to navy
Add an inline style on the <p> with color red

commonMistakes.msg: The <style> tag is for internal CSS. To link an external file, use <link rel="stylesheet" href="mystyle.css">.
The type="text/css" attribute is no longer needed in HTML5. Just use <link rel="stylesheet" href="mystyle.css">.

icons.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Add the Font Awesome icon library: insert a <link> with rel "stylesheet" and href "https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css"
  2. Use the library: add the class fa fa-cloud to the first <i> element
  3. Add the class fa fa-heart to the second <i> element
  4. Style the icons: set .fa font-size to 36px and color to #04AA6D
icons.starter:
Cloud:

Heart:
icons.solution:
Cloud:

Heart:

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Add the Font Awesome <link> stylesheet
Add class fa fa-cloud to the first icon
Add class fa fa-heart to the second icon

commonMistakes.msg: Use the full CDN URL for Font Awesome: https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/4.7.0/css/font-awesome.min.css
Font Awesome icons need both the base class and the icon class: class="fa fa-cloud", not just class="fa-cloud".

image_gallery.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set .gallery display to flex and flex-wrap to wrap
  2. Set .gallery-item margin to 5px and width to 180px
  3. Add a .gallery-item img rule: set width to 100%
image_gallery.starter:
Cinque Terre

Forest

Northern Lights
image_gallery.solution:
Cinque Terre

Forest

Northern Lights

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: This gallery uses display: flex instead of float. Set display: flex on the .gallery container.

image_sprites.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .icon background-image to url('https://www.w3schools.com/css/img_navsprites.gif')
  2. Set the .icon width to 43px and height to 44px
  3. Set the .icon-next background-position to -91px 0

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use a negative value: background-position: -91px 0. Negative values shift the sprite image to the left to reveal the next icon.

image_transparency.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .thumbnail opacity to 0.6
  2. On hover, restore full visibility: set .thumbnail:hover opacity to 1.0
  3. Set the .overlay-text background-color to rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5)
image_transparency.starter: Semi-transparent background
image_transparency.solution: Semi-transparent background

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use background-color: rgba(0,0,0,0.5) instead of opacity. RGBA makes only the background transparent, while opacity affects the entire element including text.

imagecenter.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Center the first image using margin: set .margin-center display to block and margin to 0 auto
  2. Add a .flex-center rule: set display to flex and justify-content to center (centers its child image)
imagecenter.starter:
Method 1: margin auto

Method 2: flexbox
imagecenter.solution:
Method 1: margin auto

Method 2: flexbox

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: This challenge uses margin: 0 auto and flexbox to center images, not text-align.

imagefilters.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .grayscale filter to grayscale(100%)
  2. Set the .blur filter to blur(3px)
  3. On hover, remove the filter: set img:hover filter to none

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Include the unit: blur(3px). The blur() function requires a pixel value.

imagemodal.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make the overlay cover the full screen: set .modal position to fixed, and top, left, width, height all to cover the viewport (0, 0, 100%, 100%)
  2. Set the .modal background-color to rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)
  3. Center the modal image: set .modal img display to block and margin to auto

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use position: fixed for the modal overlay. Fixed positioning keeps it in place even when scrolling.

imageshapes.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make the avatar circular: set .avatar border-radius to 50%
  2. Clip the banner to a polygon: set .banner-img clip-path to polygon(50% 0%, 100% 50%, 50% 100%, 0% 50%)

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use border-radius: 50% for the circular avatar. Use clip-path: polygon() for the angled banner shape.

important.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. In the .highlight rule, set color to red !important to override the ID selector
  2. In the .highlight rule, set background-color to yellow !important
important.starter:
Important Heading

The !important rule overrides even the ID selector.
important.solution:
Important Heading

The !important rule overrides even the ID selector.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Add !important after the value: color: red !important;. Without it, the ID selector wins.

inheritance.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Force the link to inherit the parent's color: set .card a color to inherit
  2. Reset the border on .card p to its default: set border to initial
inheritance.starter:
CSS Inheritance

Links normally don't inherit color. Use inherit to force it.

This link inherits the card color
inheritance.solution:
CSS Inheritance

Links normally don't inherit color. Use inherit to force it.

This link inherits the card color

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use color: inherit instead of hardcoding the parent's color. inherit automatically uses the parent's value.

link.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Style a:link with color #04AA6D and text-decoration none
  2. Style a:visited with color gray
  3. Add an a:hover rule: set color to #028a4e and text-decoration to underline
link.starter:
Styled Links

W3Schools

CSS Tutorial
link.solution:
Styled Links

W3Schools

CSS Tutorial

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Style specific link states like a:link, a:visited, and a:hover instead of just a.

list.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the ul list-style-type to none (remove bullets)
  2. Set the ul padding to 0
  3. Add a li rule: set padding to 8px 16px, background-color to #f1f1f1, and margin-bottom to 2px
list.starter:
Navigation Menu

  • Home
  • About
  • Services
  • Contact
list.solution:
Navigation Menu

  • Home
  • About
  • Services
  • Contact

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use list-style-type: none to specifically remove the bullet markers.

margin.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .box margin-top to 30px and margin-bottom to 30px (individual sides)
  2. Set the .box margin-left to 50px
  3. Add a .centered rule: set width to 300px and margin to 0 auto (auto centers horizontally)
margin.starter:
This box uses individual margin sides.
This box is centered with margin auto.
margin.solution:
This box uses individual margin sides.
This box is centered with margin auto.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use margin: 0 auto to center horizontally. Just margin: auto adds auto margins on all four sides.
This challenge asks for individual margin properties (margin-top, margin-bottom, margin-left), not the shorthand.

math_functions.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .sidebar width using calc: calc(100% / 3)
  2. Set the .content width using calc: calc(100% - 100% / 3)
  3. Set the .content padding using calc: calc(1rem + 10px)
math_functions.starter:
Sidebar
Main Content
math_functions.solution:
Sidebar
Main Content

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use calc(100% / 3) instead of 33%. The calc() function gives an exact one-third value.

navbar.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Style the navbar: set .navbar background-color to dimgray and overflow to hidden
  2. Style the links: set .navbar a float to left, color to white, and padding to 14px 16px
  3. Set .navbar a text-decoration to none
  4. Set .navbar a:hover background-color to #04AA6D
navbar.starter:
Home About Services Contact

Welcome

This is a horizontal navigation bar.
navbar.solution:
Home About Services Contact

Welcome

This is a horizontal navigation bar.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use float: left for the horizontal navbar pattern. The overflow: hidden on the container clears the floats.

objectfit.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .cover object-fit to cover (fills the box, may crop)
  2. Set the .contain object-fit to contain (fits inside, may letterbox)
objectfit.starter:
Cover (crops to fill)

Contain (fits inside)
objectfit.solution:
Cover (crops to fill)

Contain (fits inside)

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use cover or contain. fill stretches the image and distorts it.

objectposition.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .hero-img object-fit to cover
  2. Set the .hero-img object-position to center top (focus on the top of the image)
  3. Set the .profile-img object-fit to cover and object-position to 80% 20%

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use object-position: center top for the hero image to focus on the top portion of the image.

outline.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .solid outline-style to solid and outline-color to #04AA6D
  2. Set the .dashed outline using the shorthand: 3px dashed red
  3. Add a .offset rule: set outline to 3px solid dimgray and outline-offset to 10px
outline.starter:
Solid green outline (individual properties)
Dashed red outline (shorthand)
Outline with 10px offset from border
outline.solution:
Solid green outline (individual properties)
Dashed red outline (shorthand)
Outline with 10px offset from border

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Set .dashed outline to 3px dashed red

commonMistakes.msg: Use outline, not border. Outlines are drawn outside the border and don't affect layout.

overflow.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .scroll-box overflow to auto (scrollbar only when needed)
  2. Set the .clip-box overflow to hidden (clip overflowing content)
overflow.starter:
This box has a lot of content. When it overflows, a scrollbar appears automatically. This is useful for content areas with variable length.

This box clips any content that overflows. The extra content is hidden and not accessible to the user.
overflow.solution:
This box has a lot of content. When it overflows, a scrollbar appears automatically. This is useful for content areas with variable length.

This box clips any content that overflows. The extra content is hidden and not accessible to the user.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use overflow: auto instead of scroll. auto only shows scrollbars when content actually overflows.

padding.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .box1 padding using individual sides: padding-top 20px, padding-right 30px, padding-bottom 20px, padding-left 30px
  2. Add a .box2 rule: set the same padding using the 4-value shorthand: 20px 30px 20px 30px
padding.starter:
Individual padding sides

Padding shorthand (same result)
padding.solution:
Individual padding sides

Padding shorthand (same result)

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: For .box1, use individual properties (padding-top, padding-right, etc.), not the shorthand.

performance.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Replace the * universal selector with .card (target only what's needed)
  2. Replace the four border properties with the shorthand: border: 1px solid lightgray
  3. Replace the four padding properties with the shorthand: padding: 15px 20px
performance.starter:
Optimized CSS

Use shorthand properties and specific selectors.
performance.solution:
Optimized CSS

Use shorthand properties and specific selectors.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Replace the * universal selector with .card. The universal selector applies styles to every element, which is slow.

position.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .container position to relative (establishes positioning context)
  2. Set the .badge position to absolute, top to -10px, and right to -10px
position.starter:
3

Notifications
position.solution:
3

Notifications

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: The badge needs position: absolute to position it relative to the container. The container needs position: relative.

position_offset.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the .btn position to fixed
  2. Set bottom to 20px and right to 20px
position_offset.starter:
Fixed Position

The button should stay fixed in the bottom-right corner.
position_offset.solution:
Fixed Position

The button should stay fixed in the bottom-right corner.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use position: fixed instead of absolute. Fixed positions the element relative to the viewport, so it stays in place even when scrolling.

pseudo_classes.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the button background-color to #04AA6D, color to white, and border to none
  2. Add a button:hover rule: set background-color to #028a4e
  3. Add an input:focus rule: set outline to 2px solid #04AA6D
  4. Add a p:first-child rule: set font-weight to bold
pseudo_classes.starter:
This first paragraph should be bold (first-child).

This paragraph is normal.
pseudo_classes.solution:
This first paragraph should be bold (first-child).

This paragraph is normal.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: The challenge asks for :first-child, not :first-of-type. They behave differently when mixed element types are present.

pseudo_elements.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Add a p::first-letter rule: set font-size to 200%, color to #04AA6D, and float to left (creates a drop cap)
  2. Add a h2::before rule: set content to "\2605 " (a star) and color to gold
  3. Add a h2::after rule: set content to " \2605" and color to gold
pseudo_elements.starter:
Featured Article

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam.
pseudo_elements.solution:
Featured Article

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Add h2::before with content and color gold
Add h2::after with content and color gold

commonMistakes.msg: Use double colon ::before, ::after, ::first-letter for pseudo-elements. Single colon is the old CSS2 syntax.

rwd_grid.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the universal selector * box-sizing to border-box
  2. Set .grid-container display to grid
  3. Set .grid-container grid-template-areas to: 'header header' 'menu content' 'footer footer'
  4. Set .grid-container gap to 10px
rwd_grid.starter:
My Page

Menu

Main content goes here.

Footer
rwd_grid.solution:
My Page

Menu

Main content goes here.

Footer

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: This layout uses display: grid with grid-template-areas instead of floats.

rwd_images.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make all images responsive: set img max-width to 100% and height to auto
  2. Make the hero background responsive: set .hero background-size to cover
  3. Set .hero background-position to center
rwd_images.starter: Responsive Images
rwd_images.solution: Responsive Images

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use max-width: 100% instead of width: 100%. max-width allows the image to shrink but never exceed its natural size.

rwd_intro.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set .wrapper max-width to 960px and margin to 0 auto
  2. Make images responsive: set img max-width to 100% and height to auto
  3. Use relative font sizing: set h1 font-size to 2.5vw
rwd_intro.starter:
Responsive Web Design

Resize the browser to see the responsive effect.
rwd_intro.solution:
Responsive Web Design

Resize the browser to see the responsive effect.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use max-width: 100% for images, not width: 100%. max-width prevents the image from exceeding its natural size.

rwd_mediaqueries.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. For screens up to 600px: set .column width to 100% inside @media (max-width: 600px)
  2. For screens 601px to 900px: set .column width to 50% inside @media (max-width: 900px)
rwd_mediaqueries.starter:
Col 1
Col 2
Col 3
Col 4
rwd_mediaqueries.solution:
Col 1
Col 2
Col 3
Col 4

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use max-width for mobile-first breakpoints. max-width: 600px targets screens 600px and smaller.

rwd_videos.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Make the video responsive: set video max-width to 100% and height to auto

Using max-width instead of width ensures the video scales down on small screens but never scales up larger than its original size.
rwd_videos.starter: Responsive Video
rwd_videos.solution: Responsive Video

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use max-width: 100% instead of width: 100%. With max-width, the video will scale down but never stretch larger than its original size.

rwd_viewport.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Add the viewport <meta> tag in the <head> section (set width to device-width and initial-scale to 1.0)
  2. Prevent the image from overflowing: set img max-width to 100% and height to auto
  3. Make the content area responsive: set .content width to 100%
rwd_viewport.starter:
Responsive Page

This page uses the viewport meta tag and relative widths to look good on all devices.
rwd_viewport.solution:
Responsive Page

This page uses the viewport meta tag and relative widths to look good on all devices.

requirements.label: Add the viewport <meta> tag with width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0
Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Avoid fixed pixel widths on images. Use max-width: 100% so the image scales down on smaller screens without overflowing.
Use width: 100% instead of a fixed pixel width. Fixed widths can cause horizontal scrolling on small screens.

selectors.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Use an element selector to set all p elements color to dimgray
  2. Use a class selector to set .highlight background-color to yellow
  3. Use an id selector to set #intro font-size to 20px
selectors.starter:
Welcome to CSS selectors.

This is a normal paragraph.

This paragraph is highlighted.
selectors.solution:
Welcome to CSS selectors.

This is a normal paragraph.

This paragraph is highlighted.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Set all p elements color to dimgray

commonMistakes.msg: Use #intro (id selector with #) not .intro (class selector with .).

specificity.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Use the higher-specificity selector p.text to set color to blue
  2. Use the even higher-specificity selector #main to set color to red
specificity.starter: What color am I?
specificity.solution: What color am I?

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Use p.text to set color to blue
Use #main to set color to red

commonMistakes.msg: Use p.text (element + class) for higher specificity than just .text.

supports_rule.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Create an @supports (display: grid) feature query
  2. Inside the query, set .container display to grid and grid-template-columns to 1fr 1fr 1fr
  3. Set .container gap to 10px inside the @supports block
supports_rule.starter:
1
2
3
supports_rule.solution:
1
2
3

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Include the feature condition: @supports (display: grid) { ... }. The parentheses with the property test are required.

table.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set table border-collapse to collapse and width to 100%
  2. Add borders: set th, td border to 1px solid lightgray and padding to 12px
  3. Style the header: set th background-color to #04AA6D, color to white, and text-align to left
  4. Add zebra stripes: set tr:nth-child(even) background-color to #f2f2f2
table.starter:
NameAgeCity
Alice28London
Bob34Paris
Carol22Berlin
Dave45Madrid
table.solution:
NameAgeCity
Alice28London
Bob34Paris
Carol22Berlin
Dave45Madrid

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag
Set th, td border to 1px solid lightgray

commonMistakes.msg: Use border-collapse: collapse to merge cell borders into single lines. separate keeps double borders.

text.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the body color to dimgray (default text color for the page)
  2. Set the h1 color to green
  3. Add a .highlight rule: set background-color to yellow and color to red
text.starter:
Text Color

The body color sets the default text color for the whole page.

You can also combine text color with background color for emphasis.
text.solution:
Text Color

The body color sets the default text color for the whole page.

You can also combine text color with background color for emphasis.

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use background-color (not background) to set just the background color.

units.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Set the html font-size to 16px (base for rem units)
  2. Set the h1 font-size to 2rem (relative to root)
  3. Set the .hero height to 50vh
units.starter: Hero Section
units.solution: Hero Section

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use rem (root em) instead of em. rem is relative to the root html element, while em is relative to the parent.

website_layout.intro:
Inside the editor, complete the following steps:
  1. Style the header: set background-color to dimgray, color to white, padding to 20px, and text-align to center
  2. Style the footer: set background-color to dimgray, color to white, padding to 10px, and text-align to center
  3. Style the main: set max-width to 800px, margin to 0 auto, and padding to 20px
website_layout.starter:
My Website

Welcome

This is the main content area.

Footer © 2025
website_layout.solution:
My Website

Welcome

This is the main content area.

Footer © 2025

requirements.label: Write CSS inside the <style> tag

commonMistakes.msg: Use margin: 0 auto to center the main content - this sets top/bottom to 0 and left/right to auto.
